# Data Model Checklist: Return a Low/High Range

**Purpose**: Validate data model requirements completeness and alignment with the feature spec.
**Created**: 2026-03-10
**Feature**: `/home/ayra/ECE_493/Capstone-Test-Repo/specs/014-low-high-range/spec.md`

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are all data items listed in the spec explicitly defined with fields and types? [Completeness, Spec §Data Involved]
- [x] CHK002 Are fields defined for range metadata, interval level, and warnings? [Completeness, Spec §Functional Requirements]
- [x] CHK003 Are fields defined for range-unavailable and range-adjusted outcomes? [Completeness, Spec §Alternate Flows, Spec §Exception / Error Flows]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK004 Are constraints for range validity (low ≤ estimate ≤ high, non-negative low, max width) represented? [Clarity, Spec §Functional Requirements]
- [x] CHK005 Is consistency across repeated requests represented in the model or metadata? [Clarity, Spec §Functional Requirements]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK006 Do acceptance scenarios and functional requirements agree on range availability and warnings? [Consistency, Spec §Acceptance Scenarios, Spec §Functional Requirements]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK007 Are data representations defined for range computation failure and internal error outcomes? [Coverage, Spec §Alternate Flows, Spec §Exception / Error Flows]
